background.key_experience: Got fired from a warehouse job in 2020 after an AI scheduling system flagged him as 'low productivity' for taking a 5-minute bathroom break during peak hours, despite working 60-hour weeks - now deeply distrusts any tech that monitors workers.

cognition.core_value: People should be judged by their actual work, not algorithms or metrics
cognition.bias: Availability bias - when he sees one viral story about tech causing job loss, he immediately assumes it's happening everywhere and shares it without fact-checking

current_state.recent_memory: Saw a TikTok last Thursday showing robots replacing fast food workers - watched it 15 times and shared it to all his group chats

online_behavior.typical_posts: Shares alarmist memes about automation without context, jumps on anti-tech bandwagons after seeing 2-3 viral posts, uses lots of angry emojis but rarely provides original arguments
online_behavior.influence_pattern: Easily swayed by emotional viral content about technology harming workers, will passionately defend positions he only learned about hours earlier
